title: podman is not requesting the right scope for the catalog during search
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When attempting to use `podman search` to list the containers in a Keycloak-authenticated registry, podman fails with an error.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Stand up a kubernetes container registry using Keycloak for authentication.

2. Log into the registry.

3. Push a container to the registry.

4. Attempt to see the container with a search.

**Describe the results you received:**

```console
$ podman login --tls-verify=false https://registry.[redacted].nip.io
Username: [redacted]
Password: 
Login Succeeded!
$ podman search --tls-verify=false registry.[redacted].nip.io/[redacted]
ERRO[0003] error getting search results from v2 endpoint "registry.[redacted].nip.io": unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required 
Error: 1 error occurred:
	* couldn't search registry "registry.[redacted].nip.io": unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A list of the available containers.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Happens consistently on both EL8.6 and Fedora 37 versions.

```console
$ podman search --tls-verify=false registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/ --log-level=debug
...
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for registry.[redacted].nip.io:80 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /run/user/1000/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/registry.[redacted].nip.io:80 
DEBU[0000] trying to talk to v2 search endpoint         
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/v2/ 
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/v2/ err Get "https://registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/v2/": http: server gave HTTP response to HTTPS client (&url.Error{Op:"Get", URL:"https://registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/v2/", Err:(*errors.errorString)(0xc0008aea60)}) 
DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/v2/ 
DEBU[0000] Ping http://registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/v2/ status 401 
DEBU[0000] GET https://auth.[redacted].nip.io/auth/realms/[redacted]/protocol/docker-v2/auth?account=[redacted]-admin&service=DockerRegistry 
DEBU[0000] GET http://registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/v2/_catalog 
ERRO[0000] error getting search results from v2 endpoint "registry.[redacted].nip.io:80": unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required 
Error: 1 error occurred:
	* couldn't search registry "registry.[redacted].nip.io:80": unable to retrieve auth token: invalid username/password: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required
```

As tcpdump show (this is the packets for the ping line above Ping http://registry.[redacted].nip.io:80/v2/ status 401 ): 

```
GET /v2/ HTTP/1.1
Host: registry.[redacted].nip.io:80
User-Agent: containers/5.21.1 (github.com/containers/image)
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Accept-Encoding: gzip
Connection: close
13:18:32.097069 IP 10.240.0.100.http > 10.240.0.36.35710: Flags [.], ack 211, win 235, options [nop,nop,TS val 1996761404 ecr 3863276933], length 0
13:18:32.098923 IP 10.240.0.100.http > 10.240.0.36.35710: Flags [P.], seq 1:525, ack 211, win 235, options [nop,nop,TS val 1996761406 ecr 3863276933], length 524: HTTP: HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
Content-Length: 87
Connection: close
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Www-Authenticate: Bearer realm="https://auth.[redacted].nip.io/auth/realms/[redacted]/protocol/docker-v2/auth",service="DockerRegistry"
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Date: Mon, 05 Sep 2022 13:18:32 GMT
X-Kong-Upstream-Latency: 2
X-Kong-Proxy-Latency: 0
Via: kong/2.8.1
```

At this time podman is requesting a token to https://auth.[redacted].nip.io/auth/realms/[redacted]/protocol/docker-v2/auth", note that there is no scope, the issue is that podman is keeping the token for the request to /v2/_catalog like tcpdump log shown in these packets : 

```
GET /v2/_catalog HTTP/1.1
Host: registry.[redacted].nip.io:80
User-Agent: containers/5.21.1 (github.com/containers/image)
Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCIgOiAiSldUIiwia2lkIiA6ICI3RDVVOjVRUzI6WUNDQjpaT1I2Ok5JN086VkU2TDpJQlNCOlRJNlk6QVNIMzpNTkxVOkJTSFY6SkRNSCJ9..Azmt5qJiESD2kJ4MFEgXdlUgRTgxZBRWzlh9_D1TWwpPc45e33bNSX28XTIMNqyYHK1acUNW15HUUbGFbNR5dluFK7PE6YZcsQKM-34OzNusTAOzZWEdRzU-8NzXyEQciBxM-8z3QortYbFhyInzs86GBKknWxLt86PojeYViufG_IGSrTL2_8ekhsao69i9AfKQj1Tju-7Mj5-Ti0-XAa4pbuEZdBjcx1CGUPu_w03CV2JEmyj_0ZdTQDdLBR-Q6akdJ1FYfJNbo8kp9cHL2Gak2wpG-o09NxxpTW4q84Ys6Eeci-9QiIBEFA9FwM1XIngAIgOOAsFdoE5z_bHFMA
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Accept-Encoding: gzip
Connection: close
13:18:32.126589 IP 10.240.0.100.http > 10.240.0.36.35714: Flags [.], ack 1057, win 243, options [nop,nop,TS val 1478732270 ecr 3863276963], length 0
13:18:32.128169 IP 10.240.0.100.http > 10.240.0.36.35714: Flags [P.], seq 1:638, ack 1057, win 243, options [nop,nop,TS val 1478732272 ecr 3863276963], length 637: HTTP: HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
HTTP/1.1 401 Unauthorized
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8
Content-Length: 145
Connection: close
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Www-Authenticate: Bearer realm="https://auth.[redacted].nip.io/auth/realms/[redacted]/protocol/docker-v2/auth",service="DockerRegistry",scope="registry:catalog:*",error="insufficient_scope"
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Date: Mon, 05 Sep 2022 13:18:32 GMT
X-Kong-Upstream-Latency: 1
X-Kong-Proxy-Latency: 0
Via: kong/2.8.1

{"errors":[{"code":"UNAUTHORIZED","message":"authentication required","detail":[{"Type":"registry","Class":"","Name":"catalog","Action":"*"}]}]}
```

Finally the registry send as part of the 401 response the right scope (scope="registry:catalog:*") that podman should ask with the token, but instead podman consider the credentials as not valid and abort without asking the new token with the right scope as expected.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```console
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.17.12
Built:        Tue Aug  2 07:53:14 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```console
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3
Built:        Wed Jun 15 08:33:05 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```console
$ podman info
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.26.2
  cgroupControllers: []
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.1.2-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.1.2, commit: 98e028a5804809ccb49bc099c0d53adc43ef8cc4'
  cpuUtilization:
    idlePercent: 99.12
    systemPercent: 0.21
    userPercent: 0.67
  cpus: 1
  distribution:
    distribution: '"rocky"'
    version: "8.6"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: [redacted]
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 4.18.0-372.19.1.el8_6.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: k8s-file
  memFree: 438095872
  memTotal: 2068545536
  networkBackend: cni
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.1.3-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.1.3
      spec: 1.0.2-dev
      go: go1.17.12
      libseccomp: 2.5.2
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /tmp/podman-run-1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: true
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.2.0-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.2.0
      commit: 656041d45cfca7a4176f6b7eed9e4fe6c11e8383
      libslirp: 4.4.0
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.2
  swapFree: 6182666240
  swapTotal: 6291451904
  uptime: 42h 59m 55.53s (Approximately 1.75 days)
plugins:
  log:
  - k8s-file
  - none
  - passthrough
  - journald
  network:
  - bridge
  - macvlan
  - ipvlan
  volume:
  - local
registries:
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.redhat.io
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /home/iqube/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/iqube/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphRootAllocated: 64385212416
  graphRootUsed: 18741260288
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp
  imageStore:
    number: 1
  runRoot: /tmp/podman-run-1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/iqube/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 4.1.1
  Built: 1659426794
  BuiltTime: Tue Aug  2 07:53:14 2022
  GitCommit: ""

  OsArch: linux/amd64

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```console
$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.1.1-2.module+el8.6.0+997+05c9d812.x86_64
```

```console
$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.1.1-1.fc36.aarch64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

I'm working on replicating with 4.2 from Fedora rawhide, currently building.

I don't see anything pertinent in the Troubleshooting Guide.


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Both clients in a KVM environment; one was x86_64, the other aarch64.

comments.body:
I just discovered that podman 4.2 is in fc36 now, so I went ahead and replicated the results with that. So I think this confirms the issue persists.

```console
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 08:43:11 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64

$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.2.0-2.fc36.aarch64
```
This is very likely a duplicate of #13369, recently fixed in c/image https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1640 .
So this should be fixed in podman 4.3 which is about to be cut for RC1.
Great! As soon as that RC is released I'll try it out and report back.
Closing as https://github.com/containers/image/pull/1640 has been merged into Podman.
@rhatdan where can I get the 4.3 RC1 to test this fix?
Not being cut until Monday/Tuesday next week.

Hi, is there any update on this? I also have this issue when using Harbor as registry. My podman version:
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.7
Built:        Wed Oct 26 21:23:47 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
@HansVenema, you need to update to Podman v4.3 and above.
Okay, thanks! I will ask my infrastructure department when the update is applied and test it again.
